title: Anharmonic phonon coupling enabled by local inversion symmetry breaking at domain walls in ferroelastic
authors: Seyyed Jabbar Mousavi, Vivek Unikandanunni, Niccolo Sellati, Paolo Barone, Martina Basini, Steven L. Johnson, Andrey Shalit, Peter Hamm, Mattia Udina, Thomas Feurer
abstract: In ferroelastic materials, spontaneous symmetry breaking leads to the formation of twin domains. Although the bulk crystal typically remains centrosymmetric, inversion symmetry can be locally broken at the domain walls, potentially changing phonon selection rules and enabling local anharmonic phonon coupling. Here we report direct evidence of such anharmonic coupling in ferroelastic LaAlO$_3$ using two-dimensional Raman-terahertz spectroscopy. We attribute the cross-peaks observed in the two-dimensional spectra to both mechanical and electrical anharmonicity between the $A_{1g}$ Raman-active phonon and the $E_g$ phonon, which acquires finite infrared activity through local inversion symmetry breaking at ferroelastic domain walls. These findings provide new insight into the complex lattice dynamics of ferroelastic materials and highlight the potential of two-dimensional Raman-terahertz spectroscopy to uncover subtle symmetry breaking through the detection of intrinsically weak anharmonic signals.
summary: The researchers used 2D Raman-terahertz spectroscopy to demonstrate that ferroelastic domain walls in LaAlO3 break inversion symmetry, enabling previously hidden anharmonic phonon coupling. This discovery provides a way to probe subtle symmetry-breaking effects in complex materials using nonlinear spectroscopy.
main_problem: Investigating whether local inversion symmetry breaking at ferroelastic domain walls enables anharmonic phonon coupling that is otherwise absent in the centrosymmetric bulk.
main_result: Direct evidence of mechanical and electrical anharmonic coupling between A1g and Eg phonons was observed in LaAlO3, driven by local symmetry breaking at domain walls.
method: Two-dimensional Raman-terahertz spectroscopy was used to detect cross-peaks in the spectra.
model_or_system: The physical system is ferroelastic LaAlO3, specifically focusing on the lattice dynamics at twin domain walls where inversion symmetry is locally broken.
key_observables: Cross-peaks in two-dimensional Raman-terahertz spectra, A1g Raman-active phonon, and Eg infrared-active phonon.
important_parameters: Local inversion symmetry breaking at domain walls; mechanical and electrical anharmonicity.
main_assumptions: The bulk crystal is assumed to be centrosymmetric, with symmetry breaking restricted to the domain walls.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the concept of local symmetry breaking in ferroelastic domain walls, presents experimental evidence using 2D Raman-THz spectroscopy, and attributes the observed signals to specific phonon coupling mechanisms.
